A01.D: Learning from labeled input-output pairs using explicit supervision signals

A02.D: Learning structure or representations from unlabeled data without explicit supervision

A03.D: Learning through interaction with an environment via reward signals, including model-based, model-free, multi-agent, and inverse RL

A04.D: Learning from a mix of labeled and unlabeled data to reduce annotation requirements

A05.D: Learning representations using automatically generated supervision from the data itself, including contrastive, masked, and predictive approaches

A06.D: Distributed learning across decentralized data sources without sharing raw data

A07.D: Learning to generalize to new tasks with very few or no labeled examples

A08.D: Iteratively selecting the most informative samples for labeling to reduce annotation cost

A09.D: Learning incrementally from a stream of data arriving sequentially over time

A10.D: Learning new tasks over time without catastrophically forgetting previously learned knowledge

A11.D: Jointly learning multiple related tasks to improve generalization across all of them

A12.D: Learning behaviors by mimicking demonstrations from an expert agent

A13.D: Learning to learn, enabling rapid adaptation to new tasks with minimal data

A14.D: Applying knowledge learned from one domain or task to improve performance on another

A15.D: Adapting a model trained on a source domain to perform well on a different target domain

B01.D: Sequence models based on self-attention, including variants like BERT, GPT, and Vision Transformers

B02.D: Feedforward networks using local convolutional filters, primarily for structured grid data like images

B03.D: Sequential models with hidden state memory, including LSTM and GRU variants

B04.D: Neural networks that operate on graph-structured data via message passing between nodes

B05.D: Architectures that route inputs to specialized sub-networks to increase model capacity efficiently

B06.D: Generative models that learn to reverse a gradual noising process to produce samples

B07.D: Variational Autoencoders that learn latent representations by optimizing an evidence lower bound

B08.D: Generative Adversarial Networks that train a generator and discriminator in an adversarial game

B09.D: Ensemble and decision tree methods including Random Forests, XGBoost, and gradient boosting

B10.D: Models representing joint distributions via graph-structured conditional independencies

B11.D: Neural networks defined by continuous-time dynamics via ordinary differential equations

B12.D: Neural networks that incorporate physical laws and constraints into their training objectives

B13.D: Networks designed to respect geometric symmetries and transformations in data

B14.D: Biologically inspired networks that communicate via discrete spike events over time

B15.D: Hybrid models combining neural learning with symbolic reasoning or logic

B16.D: Sequence models using structured state-space representations, including Mamba and S4

C01.D: First- and second-order gradient-based methods for minimizing training objectives, including SGD and Adam

C02.D: Techniques to reduce overfitting by constraining model complexity, including dropout and weight decay

C03.D: Methods for stabilizing activations during training, including BatchNorm, LayerNorm, and RMSNorm

C04.D: Automated methods for discovering optimal neural network architectures

C05.D: Automated selection of model types and hyperparameters to maximize performance, including Bayesian optimization approaches

C06.D: Transformations applied to training data to artificially increase diversity and improve generalization

C07.D: Strategies for training models across multiple devices or machines simultaneously

C08.D: Optimization using population-based, biologically inspired search and selection procedures

C09.D: Crafting or learning objective functions to better align training with task goals

C10.D: Methods for managing gradients during training, including clipping, accumulation, and checkpointing

C11.D: Training with reduced floating-point precision to accelerate computation and reduce memory usage

C12.D: Converting raw inputs into discrete tokens or normalized representations, including BPE and SentencePiece

D01.D: Adapting a pre-trained model to a downstream task using labeled examples

D02.D: Fine-tuning methods that update only a small subset of parameters, including LoRA, adapters, and QLoRA

D03.D: Aligning model outputs with human or AI preferences using reinforcement learning feedback

D04.D: Alignment methods that optimize preference data directly without a separate reward model, including DPO variants

D05.D: Reducing model weight or activation precision to lower memory and inference cost

D06.D: Removing weights, neurons, or attention heads to reduce model size and computation

D07.D: Transferring knowledge from a large teacher model to a smaller student model

D08.D: Designing or optimizing input prompts to steer model behavior without weight updates

D09.D: Adapting model behavior through examples provided in the prompt without gradient updates

D10.D: Combining multiple trained models into a single model to aggregate capabilities

D11.D: Using additional computation at inference to improve output quality, including chain-of-thought and tree search

D12.D: Augmenting model generation by retrieving relevant external documents or knowledge at inference time

D13.D: Techniques for extending a model's effective context window beyond its training length

D14.D: Incrementally updating a deployed model on new data while preserving existing capabilities

E01.D: Solving tasks requiring mathematical, logical, causal, or commonsense inference

E02.D: Producing coherent sequences, images, or structured outputs conditionally or unconditionally

E03.D: Assigning discrete labels to inputs from a fixed set of categories

E04.D: Predicting structured outputs such as sequences, trees, or graphs

E05.D: Finding and ranking relevant items from large collections in response to a query

E06.D: Identifying inputs that deviate significantly from expected or normal patterns

E07.D: Predicting future values in sequential temporal data

E08.D: Suggesting relevant items to users based on preferences and behavior

E09.D: Automatically producing executable code or programs from natural language or specifications

E10.D: Selecting sequences of actions to achieve goals in structured or open-ended environments

E11.D: Unexpected or qualitatively new abilities that arise in models as scale or complexity increases

E12.D: Systems that autonomously perceive, plan, and act, including using external tools and APIs

E13.D: Processing or generating content that spans multiple modalities such as text, image, and audio

E14.D: Converting text between languages and transferring knowledge across linguistic boundaries

E15.D: Condensing long documents or conversations into shorter, faithful representations

E16.D: Producing accurate answers to natural language questions from context or knowledge

E17.D: Managing multi-turn natural language interactions between a model and a user

F01.D: ML applications targeting text understanding, generation, and language structure

F02.D: ML applications targeting visual data including images, video, and 3D scenes

F03.D: ML applications targeting spoken language, music, and general audio signals

F04.D: Applications combining multiple modalities such as vision-language and audio-visual systems

F05.D: ML applied to physical agents that must perceive, decide, and act in the real world

F06.D: ML applied to natural sciences including biology, chemistry, physics, and climate modeling

F07.D: ML applied to financial markets, economic modeling, trading, and risk analysis

F08.D: ML applied to medical diagnosis, treatment, clinical records, and health outcomes

F09.D: ML applied to personalized learning, tutoring systems, and educational analytics

F10.D: ML applied to legal document analysis, case prediction, and regulatory compliance

F11.D: ML applied to games and strategic decision environments including board and video games

F12.D: ML applied to self-driving vehicles, drones, and other autonomous physical systems

F13.D: ML applied to threat detection, intrusion detection, and attack/defense in digital systems

F14.D: ML applied to languages with limited data resources and multilingual settings

G01.D: Curated evaluation datasets and standardized tasks for measuring model performance

G02.D: Developing and validating quantitative measures of model quality and behavior

G03.D: Formal mathematical analysis of learning algorithms and their properties

G04.D: Theoretical study of why and how models generalize beyond their training data

G05.D: Mathematical analysis of convergence, stability, and landscape properties of training objectives

G06.D: Methods for understanding and communicating model decisions to humans

G07.D: Fine-grained analysis of internal model computations to reverse-engineer learned algorithms

G08.D: Study of the structure, geometry, and quality of learned feature spaces

G09.D: Identifying, constructing, or transforming input features to improve model performance

G10.D: Empirical analysis of how model performance changes with scale in parameters, data, and compute, including compute-optimal training

G11.D: Meta-level analysis of whether existing metrics and benchmarks actually measure what they claim

G12.D: Empirical study of unexpected capabilities arising from scale or training conditions

G13.D: Systematic empirical comparisons of multiple methods, models, or approaches

H01.D: Designing or adapting ML workloads to maximize efficiency on GPUs, TPUs, and custom accelerators

H02.D: Techniques for reducing latency and cost during model serving, including speculative decoding and batching

H03.D: Broad techniques for reducing model size including combinations of quantization, pruning, and distillation

H04.D: Practices and tools for deploying, monitoring, and versioning ML models in production

H05.D: Running ML models on resource-constrained devices such as phones and embedded systems

H06.D: Infrastructure and algorithms for coordinating ML workloads across multiple machines

H07.D: Libraries and platforms for building, training, and deploying ML models

H08.D: Automatically generating training data and managing data processing workflows

H09.D: Assessing, filtering, and cleaning datasets to ensure training data quality

H10.D: Embedding or detecting signals to identify AI-generated text, images, or audio

H11.D: Reducing memory footprint and storage requirements of models and activations

I01.D: Ensuring ML systems pursue goals consistent with human values and intentions

I02.D: Defending against or studying attacks that cause models to fail on adversarially crafted inputs

I03.D: Protecting sensitive information in training data and model outputs, including formal privacy guarantees

I04.D: Identifying and mitigating discriminatory patterns in model predictions and training data

I05.D: Measuring and communicating model confidence and predictive uncertainty

I06.D: Identifying inputs that fall outside the training distribution where model predictions are unreliable

I07.D: Studying and mitigating hidden triggers that cause malicious model behavior on specific inputs

I08.D: Reducing the environmental and energy footprint of training and deploying ML systems

I09.D: Studying and designing the interface and collaboration between humans and AI systems

I10.D: Policy, legal, and institutional frameworks for overseeing AI development and deployment

I11.D: Detecting and preventing harmful or malicious uses of ML systems

I12.D: Improving factual accuracy and reducing fabricated outputs in generative models

I13.D: Legal and technical questions around ownership and reproduction of training data and model outputs
